title: Human Agent Guide — Handling Calls in the Contact Center
lede: Your complete day-one guide as a human agent in iMash. In about 12 minutes you'll know how to log in, go Online, answer and place calls, watch the queue, read the caller's record while you talk, transfer to a teammate, a queue, or an AI agent, update the lead, and leave notes that make you look like a pro.

steps.title: Step 1 — Log in to your account
steps.content:
Everything starts at your company's login page — one browser tab is your entire phone system.

  1. Open the link your company gave you in your web browser (Chrome works best) — this is your login page.
  2. Type the Email and Password your manager sent you.
  3. Tick Remember me so you don't have to sign in every time on your own computer.
  4. Click Sign in.
💡 Forgot your password? Click Forgot password? on the login page or ask your manager to reset it — never create a second account with Sign up.

images.alt: iMash login page with email and password fields
images.caption: Your login page — enter the email and password your manager gave you, then click Sign in.

images.alt: iMash login page with email and password fields
images.caption: Your login page — enter the email and password your manager gave you, then click Sign in.

steps.title: Step 2 — Get to know your home screen
steps.content:
After you log in you land on the Contact Center — your home base all day. Here's the layout:

  • Left menu — everything you can open: Messages (SMS Messages, WhatsApp, Email), Call Center (Contact Center, Call Logs, Do Not Call), and your CRM (Accounts, Contacts, Leads). At the bottom: Account Settings and Logout.
  • Top tabs — quick switches between Contact Center, CRM and Messages, with sub-tabs like Overview, Call Logs and Do Not Call.
  • Live Monitor → On Queue (middle) — customers waiting to be connected to an agent show up here in real time.
  • Web dialer (right side) — your phone. It stays pinned open all day so you never miss a call.

images.alt: Agent home screen with the left menu, On Queue panel, and web dialer
images.caption: Your home screen: the menu on the left, the On Queue panel in the middle, and your always-on web dialer on the right.

images.alt: Agent home screen with the left menu, On Queue panel, and web dialer
images.caption: Your home screen: the menu on the left, the On Queue panel in the middle, and your always-on web dialer on the right.

steps.title: Step 3 — Meet your web dialer
steps.content:
The web dialer on the right is your phone — no headset software, no desk phone. From top to bottom:

  • A search bar (Search by name, email, or phone) — type at least two characters to find a customer in your CRM and call them straight from the result, no number-typing needed.
  • A number field and a keypad to type a phone number.
  • Call — place a phone call to the number you typed.
  • Text — send an SMS to that number instead of calling (opens a prefilled message window).
  • AI Agent — start a voice call with one of your company's AI assistants (great for testing or letting the AI handle something).
  • Teammate — call a colleague; their web dialer rings wherever they're logged in.
  • My status card — set yourself Online, On Break or Offline, with a gear icon for personal settings (next steps).

images.alt: Web dialer keypad with Call, Text, AI Agent and Teammate buttons
images.caption: Your web dialer: keypad on top, then Call, Text, AI Agent and Teammate.

images.alt: Web dialer keypad with Call, Text, AI Agent and Teammate buttons
images.caption: Your web dialer: keypad on top, then Call, Text, AI Agent and Teammate.

steps.title: Step 4 — Go Online so calls reach you
steps.content:
Your status decides whether the system rings you. Set it right and calls flow to you automatically.

  1. Find the My status card at the bottom of the dialer.
  2. Open the dropdown and pick Online — you'll now be rung for inbound calls to your groups' numbers and for calls the AI transfers to your group.
  3. Pick On Break when you step away, and Offline at the end of your shift.

You'll also see a fourth status, Busy — you can't select it yourself. The system sets it automatically while you're on a live call (Busy on a Call) or logging a call outcome afterwards (Busy — Wrap-up), so no new call rings you until you're free.

⚠️ If your status is On Break or Offline, calls will not ring you. Make going Online the first thing you do every shift.

images.alt: My status dropdown open showing Online, On Break, Offline and Busy
images.caption: Open My status and pick Online. Busy is set automatically while you're on a call or wrapping one up.

images.alt: My status dropdown open showing Online, On Break, Offline and Busy
images.caption: Open My status and pick Online. Busy is set automatically while you're on a call or wrapping one up.

steps.title: Step 5 — Set two handy options (gear icon)
steps.content:
Click the gear icon next to My status to open Web dialer settings:

  • Auto-answer — connects group calls automatically, without clicking Accept. Great for busy queues where every second counts.
  • Auto-open lead/contact — opens the caller's record the moment an incoming call matches a lead or contact, so their details are on screen before you say hello.

If you also use the iMash mobile app, the status card shows extra options: Ring my phone when the app is closed (group calls ring your phone like a normal call) and a My devices list where you can unregister old browsers or phones.

💡 Ask your manager which options they'd like your team to turn on — some groups force Auto-answer on for everyone.

images.alt: Web dialer settings popover with Auto-answer and Auto-open lead/contact switches
images.caption: Web dialer settings: Auto-answer and Auto-open lead/contact.

images.alt: Web dialer settings popover with Auto-answer and Auto-open lead/contact switches
images.caption: Web dialer settings: Auto-answer and Auto-open lead/contact.

steps.title: Step 6 — Answer an incoming call
steps.content:
When a call comes to you, a green incoming-call card pops up at the top of the dialer showing who's calling. The label tells you the type: Incoming call (a customer dialing in), AI transfer — caller waiting (the AI is handing you a live customer), Incoming transfer or Conference invite (a teammate is sending you a call or pulling you in).

  1. Click Accept (green) to take the call — or Decline if you can't.
  2. If Auto-answer is on, the call connects on its own — a chime tells you you're live, so just start talking.
  3. For a team (group) call, the first agent to Accept wins the call; everyone else's ring stops and shows "Answered by teammate". Answer quickly!

Once connected, the caller's record can open automatically on screen (see Step 8).

images.alt: The web dialer where the incoming-call card appears
images.caption: Incoming calls pop up as a green card at the top of this dialer — click Accept to connect.

images.alt: The web dialer where the incoming-call card appears
images.caption: Incoming calls pop up as a green card at the top of this dialer — click Accept to connect.

steps.title: Step 7 — Keep an eye on the queue
steps.content:
The On Queue panel in the middle of your home screen shows customers who are waiting for an agent right now — for example callers the AI parked in your group's queue, listening to hold music while the system rings the team.

  • The number next to On Queue is how many customers are waiting.
  • While you're Online, queued calls ring your dialer automatically — you don't "pick" from the queue, the system brings the next customer to you.
  • When nobody is waiting you'll see No customers on queue — that's a good thing!
💡 A growing queue count is your cue to wrap up politely and get back Online — every name on that list is a real customer holding.

images.alt: On Queue panel showing no customers waiting
images.caption: The On Queue panel — customers waiting for an agent appear here in real time.

images.alt: On Queue panel showing no customers waiting
images.caption: The On Queue panel — customers waiting for an agent appear here in real time.

steps.title: Step 8 — Read the caller's record while you talk
steps.content:
When a call connects (or when you open a lead yourself), the caller's Lead Details open so you always know who you're talking to. Look at:

  • The name, phone number and email at the top.
  • The Lead Information tab — emails, company (Account), Tags, phone numbers and timezone.
  • The other tabs — Additional Information, Call Logs, Files, Custom Data, Calendar, Email and Deals — for the full history.

Use this to greet the customer by name and pick up exactly where the last conversation left off — it feels like magic to the caller.

images.alt: Lead Details panel with tabs and contact info
images.caption: The caller's Lead Details — name, contact info, and tabs with their full history.

images.alt: Lead Details panel with tabs and contact info
images.caption: The caller's Lead Details — name, contact info, and tabs with their full history.

steps.title: Step 9 — Update the customer's stage
steps.content:
Scroll to the Lifecycle bar on the lead and click the stage that matches where the customer is now:

  • New → Contacted → Interested → Qualified → Negotiation → Won.
  • Some stages have a small arrow — click it to pick a more specific sub-stage (for example "No Answer" under Contacted).

Keeping the stage up to date tells your whole team — and your manager's reports — exactly where each customer stands.

images.alt: Lifecycle stage bar from New to Won
images.caption: The Lifecycle bar — click the stage that matches the customer after your call.

images.alt: Lifecycle stage bar from New to Won
images.caption: The Lifecycle bar — click the stage that matches the customer after your call.

steps.title: Step 10 — Write a note about the call
steps.content:
1. On the lead, find the Notes box (Add your note).
  2. Type a short, clear note about what happened — for example "Called back, wants a quote for 2 doors, follow up Friday."
  3. Press Enter to save. Your note is stamped with your name and the time, so whoever talks to this customer next knows the whole story.

On some teams, a How did the call go? window also pops up by itself right after a call ends — pick the outcome that fits, add an optional note, or click Skip. While it's open your status shows Busy — Wrap-up, so no new call rings you until you've logged the result.

💡 Good notes are the #1 thing that makes you look professional to the next agent — and to your manager.

images.alt: Notes box on the lead with Add your note field
images.caption: Add a short note about the call so the next person knows what happened.

images.alt: Notes box on the lead with Add your note field
images.caption: Add a short note about the call so the next person knows what happened.

steps.title: Step 11 — Call or transfer to a teammate, a queue, a number, or an AI
steps.content:
The Teammate button does double duty:

  • With no call active — it opens Call a teammate: pick a colleague and click Call teammate; their web dialer rings. Only teammates with their web dialer open are shown, and you can narrow the list by group or with Filter by skill… — typing in the search box matches names, skills and groups alike.
  • During a live call — the same button opens Conference / Transfer with four tabs:
    • A teammate — ring a colleague to join your call or take it over.
    • A queue — park the customer in a team's queue; they hear the group's hold music while the system rings that team's online agents until one answers.
    • A number — dial any outside phone number into the call (pick the Call from caller ID it should show).
    • AI Agent — click Add AI to call to bring an AI assistant in as another participant; hang up whenever you like to leave the AI with the customer.

For a teammate or a number, the default Add to call keeps you on the line so you can introduce everyone — a warm transfer / conference, and you can keep adding parties. Flip on Hand off & drop when they join (blind transfer) and the button becomes Transfer & leave: you drop off the moment they join. On the queue tab, Place in queue keeps you on while the queue rings; turn on Leave the call after placing in queue (the button becomes Queue & leave) to drop off right away — the AI keeps the customer company until an agent answers, so nobody is ever left in silence.

images.alt: Call a teammate popup with teammate picker
images.caption: Call a teammate — pick who should take the call; their web dialer rings. During a live call this popup adds A queue, A number and AI Agent tabs.

images.alt: Call a teammate popup with teammate picker
images.caption: Call a teammate — pick who should take the call; their web dialer rings. During a live call this popup adds A queue, A number and AI Agent tabs.

steps.title: Step 12 — Make an outbound call
steps.content:
1. Type the phone number on the keypad (or open a lead and click its phone number).
  2. Click Call.
  3. In the Start Call window, pick:
    • Caller ID (dial-out number) — the number the call is placed from; it's what the person you're calling sees.
    • AI Agent — an assistant that joins the call: it quietly transcribes the conversation in Human Mode, or handles the call itself in AI Mode.
  4. Click Call to place it — and start talking when the customer answers.

images.alt: Start Call window with Caller ID and AI Agent pickers
images.caption: Placing an outbound call — choose the Caller ID the customer will see and an AI agent, then Call.

images.alt: Start Call window with Caller ID and AI Agent pickers
images.caption: Placing an outbound call — choose the Caller ID the customer will see and an AI agent, then Call.

steps.title: Step 13 — Review a call afterwards
steps.content:
Every call you handle is saved under the customer's Call Logs tab in their Lead Details — date, type, number and duration, plus the recording and transcript when enabled. Open it any time to remind yourself what was said, or to show your manager how a call went.

That's the full loop: go Online, take the call, help the customer, update the stage, leave a note — and iMash keeps a perfect record of all of it. Welcome to the team!

images.alt: Call Logs tab on the lead showing the call history table
images.caption: The Call Logs tab keeps a record of every call with this customer.

images.alt: Call Logs tab on the lead showing the call history table
images.caption: The Call Logs tab keeps a record of every call with this customer.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Human Agent Guide — Handling Calls in the iMash Contact Center
metaDescription: Step-by-step guide for human agents: log in, go Online, answer and place calls, watch the queue, read the caller's record, transfer to a teammate, queue or AI, update the lead stage, add notes, and review calls.
